brief_document.title: Frontend runtime awareness and session tab differentiation
brief_document.purpose: The frontend currently has no concept of which runtime backs an agent session. The session API already returns runtime_kind (SessionInfoResponse at api/assistant.rs:103) but the frontend never reads it. Two model fallback strings hardcode 'claude'. The session tab in CommsInput renders identical content for both runtimes. This brief adds runtime awareness to the frontend so Tom can distinguish Norn from Claude Runner sessions and the UI adapts appropriately.
brief_document.task: Add runtime_kind to the frontend session types and surface it in the assistant hooks. Fix the two hardcoded 'claude' model fallbacks in AssistantPanel.tsx. Add a runtime indicator to the session toolbar so the active runtime is visible. The SessionStartedEvent from the WebSocket should include runtime_kind if available, and the REST session response already provides it.

requirements.title: Runtime kind in frontend session types
requirements.spec: WHEN the frontend receives session data from REST or WebSocket THE SYSTEM SHALL include a runtime_kind field ('norn' or 'claude_runner') AND it SHALL surface runtime_kind through useAssistantSession and useActiveAgentSessions hooks.
acceptance: SessionStartedEvent type in features/assistant/types.ts includes optional runtime_kind?: 'norn' | 'claude_runner' field
useAssistantSession hook exposes runtimeKind from the session_started event
REST session response type includes runtime_kind field matching the backend SessionInfoResponse
useActiveAgentSessions includes runtime_kind in the session info returned to consumers

requirements.title: Fix hardcoded model fallback strings
requirements.spec: WHEN displaying the model name for an agent session THE SYSTEM SHALL use the model field from the session data AND it SHALL NOT fall back to a hardcoded 'claude' string. WHEN the model field is null or undefined, THE SYSTEM SHALL display the runtime kind as fallback ('Norn' or 'Claude Runner').
acceptance: AssistantPanel.tsx line 118 no longer uses ?? 'claude' — uses runtime-aware fallback
AssistantPanel.tsx line 334 no longer uses ?? 'claude' — uses runtime-aware fallback
A Norn session with no model field displays 'Norn' not 'claude'
A Claude Runner session with no model field displays 'Claude Runner' not 'claude'

requirements.title: Runtime indicator in session toolbar
requirements.spec: WHEN the session toolbar renders for an active session THE SYSTEM SHALL display a runtime badge showing 'Norn' or 'Claude Runner' alongside the model and context window badges in the ProfileRow AND it SHALL use distinct visual treatment (color or icon) so the runtime is identifiable at a glance.
acceptance: Session toolbar (session-toolbar.tsx ProfileRow, around line 73-82) includes a runtime badge
Badge displays 'Norn' for Norn sessions and 'Claude Runner' for Claude Runner sessions
Badge uses distinct color: Norn sessions get a different badge color than Claude Runner
Badge is visible without expanding the profile row — it appears on the same row as model and context window badges

boundaries: SHALL NOT change the backend SessionInfoResponse or AssistantEvent types — they already include runtime_kind
SHALL NOT add Norn-specific event processing to useAssistantSession — the event stream is runtime-agnostic by design
SHALL NOT change the session toolbar layout structure — only add the runtime badge within the existing ProfileRow
verification: TypeScript build passes with no errors (bun run build)
Biome lint passes on all modified files
Norn sessions display 'Norn' runtime badge in session toolbar
Claude Runner sessions display 'Claude Runner' runtime badge
No hardcoded 'claude' fallback strings remain in AssistantPanel.tsx
runtime_kind is accessible from useAssistantSession hook

checklist.text: Frontend session types include runtime_kind field, surfaced through useAssistantSession and useActiveAgentSessions hooks
No hardcoded 'claude' model fallback strings in AssistantPanel.tsx — runtime-aware fallback instead
Session toolbar displays a runtime badge (Norn or Claude Runner) in the ProfileRow with distinct visual treatment

stories.text: As a developer, I want to see which runtime a session is using so that I can diagnose runtime-specific issues.

As a developer, I want a member-facing Norn profile that configures namespace tools and system instructions for workspace member behavior so that I can wake members as Norn sessions without manual configuration.

constraints.text: No new external dependencies. Namespace tools call service-layer functions via ServiceContext, not HTTP.
AssistantEvent is the single event type for all downstream consumers. Both runtimes produce it.
JSONL session files are Norn's source of truth for resume. PG is the UI's source of truth. Neither replaces the other.
The model sees at most 25 tools (16 standard Norn + up to 7 namespace + 2 optional web). Profile filtering reduces this in practice.
Session IDs are UUIDs shared between the JSONL filename, PG assistant_session_events key, and SessionHandle registry key.
Wake prompt delivery mechanism changes, format does not.
mod.rs contains only pub mod declarations and re-exports.
No file over 500 lines of code (excluding tests, comments, whitespace).
All errors handled via thiserror in library code. No .unwrap() or .expect() in library code.
Namespace tools implement the existing Tool trait. No changes to the trait interface.

resolved_context.intention: Norn becomes the primary runtime for all member-facing work in Meridian. Every DM, wake prompt, inbox triage, and interactive assistant session runs through Norn in-process within the server. Claude Runner stays only for legacy background task dispatch until it can be retired entirely. Member-facing sessions run as typed, observable, in-process agent loops rather than opaque subprocess invocations. Session state persists across visits without relying on a CLI binary's internal persistence. The model sees a focused set of domain tools instead of a raw shell with 175 CLI commands. This is the June 15 deadline work.

provenance.quote: Norn becomes the primary runtime for all member-facing work in Meridian. Every DM, wake prompt, inbox triage, and interactive assistant session runs through Norn in-process within the server. Claude R
